NVDA.thesis: NVIDIA remains the essential infrastructure layer for AI compute. With H100/H200 sold out and Blackwell ramping, the company is entrenched in every major hyperscaler capex cycle. At -21% from highs on macro/tariff concerns rather than fundamental deterioration, this is an attractive long-term entry. Data center revenue growth remains robust and the competitive moat (CUDA ecosystem) is extraordinarily deep.

MSFT.thesis: Microsoft is the most diversified AI infrastructure play combining Azure cloud, OpenAI partnership, and enterprise software. Azure AI workloads are growing >50% YoY. The stock is -35% from its 52-week high, pricing in excessive pessimism. Copilot integration across Office 365 creates a durable recurring revenue stream. Strong FCF generation supports continued investment in AI infrastructure.

GOOGL.thesis: Alphabet operates one of the world's largest AI infrastructure networks via its custom TPU chips and global data centers. Google Cloud is growing rapidly with AI workloads. At -21% from highs and trading at reasonable multiples relative to growth, the market is undervaluing the optionality in DeepMind, Waymo, and AI-enhanced search monetization. The selloff appears macro-driven rather than fundamental.

AMZN.thesis: Amazon Web Services is the world's largest cloud provider and is deploying massive capex into AI infrastructure including custom Trainium/Inferentia chips. The combination of AWS AI services growth, advertising tailwinds, and logistics optimization via AI makes AMZN a diversified AI beneficiary. The stock has pulled back -23% from highs on macro concerns while fundamentals remain intact.

AVGO.thesis: Broadcom is the leading custom silicon (XPU/ASIC) partner for Google, Meta, and other hyperscalers building proprietary AI chips. This business is growing explosively and provides a high-margin alternative to NVDA for inference workloads. VMware integration is progressing ahead of schedule. At -27% from 52wk highs, the market undervalues the secular AI chip design opportunity.

ARM.thesis: ARM Holdings licenses the CPU architecture that underpins virtually all mobile and an increasing share of data center AI chips. The royalty model provides high operating leverage as ASP and complexity of chips increases with AI workloads. Recent relative strength (+11.5% 1M vs QQQ -7.7%) signals the market is recognizing ARM's central role in next-generation AI silicon. Custom server chips from Apple, Amazon, Google, and Microsoft all run on ARM.

MRVL.thesis: Marvell Technology is emerging as a top-tier custom silicon provider for AI data centers, competing directly for hyperscaler ASIC business alongside Broadcom. The electro-optics and interconnect portfolio is critical for high-bandwidth AI cluster networking. The +19.7% 1M return in a brutal tape signals fundamental outperformance. At -7.6% from 52wk highs, this is one of the strongest risk/reward setups in the AI infrastructure space.

EQIX.thesis: Equinix operates the world's largest network of interconnected data centers and is a direct beneficiary of explosive AI infrastructure buildout. The REIT structure provides dividend income while the business model benefits from long-term customer contracts. Near its 52-week high, this is a defensive AI infrastructure play with lower volatility than pure-play semiconductor names.

LNG.thesis: Cheniere Energy operates the largest LNG export terminal in the US and has long-term take-or-pay contracts providing extremely visible cash flows. The structural shift in global energy markets post-Ukraine invasion has created a sustained multi-year demand tailwind for US LNG exports. The stock's outperformance in a down market signals institutional accumulation on defensive energy thesis. With the US administration explicitly supporting LNG exports and capacity expansions underway, the growth runway is long. The FCF yield is attractive and management has committed to returning capital via buybacks and dividends. This diversifies the portfolio's macro exposure away from pure AI semiconductor cyclicality.

CVX.thesis: Chevron is one of the world's highest-quality integrated energy majors with a fortress balance sheet and industry-leading capital return program. The company generates substantial FCF even at $70/bbl oil, and current oil prices support excellent economics. The stock is showing notable relative strength against a broadly weak market, suggesting institutional positioning in defensive energy as a tariff/recession hedge. Chevron's downstream operations and chemicals business provide diversification. The ~4% dividend yield provides income while we wait for the thesis to compound. The energy sector is a natural inflation hedge and diversifier against the portfolio's heavy AI technology concentration.

CIEN.thesis: Ciena is the leading provider of intelligent networking systems, software, and services and is a direct picks-and-shovels beneficiary of the AI data center buildout. Hyperscalers are spending aggressively on high-bandwidth optical networking to connect GPU clusters within and between data centers, and Ciena is a primary supplier to many of these customers. The +17.7% 1M outperformance in a brutal tape reflects genuine order book strength and consensus estimate revisions higher. The company has returned to double-digit revenue growth as its Waveserver product cycle gains traction. At -10% from its 52-week high compared to AI semis down 20-30%, the market is correctly identifying Ciena as a higher-quality, lower-volatility AI infrastructure beneficiary. The networking layer of AI infrastructure is often overlooked but is mission-critical.

META.thesis: Meta Platforms is one of the highest-quality internet businesses in the world, with 3+ billion daily active users across its family of apps and an advertising business that is structurally taking share from linear media. The company's aggressive AI investment — including its Llama models, AI recommendation systems, and Ray-Ban AI glasses — is already showing ROI through improved ad targeting and engagement metrics. At -34% from highs on tariff/macro concerns despite the business being fundamentally domestic US (minimal direct tariff exposure), the valuation is extremely attractive. Meta trades at roughly 22x forward earnings with 15-20% revenue growth guided — a significant discount to the S&P 500 multiple on an absolute basis. The $50B+ annual FCF funds massive buybacks (Meta repurchased 8% of shares outstanding in 2024) that compound per-share value even in sideways markets. Zuckerberg's clear AI vision and track record of pivoting successfully (Instagram acquisition, Reels pivot, now AI) give management high marks. This is one of the highest-conviction names in the universe at current prices.

COST.thesis: Costco Wholesale is one of the finest retail businesses ever built, with a membership-based model that creates extraordinary loyalty (renewal rates >90%), predictable recurring revenue, and a reputation for value that attracts members in good times and bad. The business is gaining market share in the current inflationary environment as consumers trade down to bulk purchasing. International expansion — particularly in Asia — provides a multi-year growth runway that is entirely self-funded by the business. The private label Kirkland brand continues to take shelf space and margin. Costco's -0.3% performance in a month when SPY fell 7.8% validates its defensive quality. While the valuation is not cheap at ~50x forward PE, Costco has consistently justified premium multiples through consistent high-single-digit revenue growth and member loyalty. This position provides crucial portfolio ballast when AI/tech faces macro headwinds.

TSM.thesis: Taiwan Semiconductor is the world's only manufacturer of leading-edge chips below 5nm, making it structurally irreplaceable for every AI semiconductor company in the portfolio and the global tech economy. • Moat: Technologically insurmountable manufacturing moat — 3nm/2nm process leadership is 2-3 years ahead of nearest competitor (Intel); switching costs are effectively infinite as no hyperscaler can abandon TSM's process without forfeiting AI chip competitiveness; scale advantages from $30B+ annual capex maintain the compounding lead. • Growth: Revenue growing 20% YoY, AI/HPC segment growing 50%+ and now represents >25% of revenue; CoWoS advanced packaging is sold out through 2025-26; TAM expanding as AI training/inference chips require increasingly advanced nodes; Arizona fab expansion (with $100B US commitment) reduces geopolitical risk premium over time. • Valuation: Trailing P/E 33x, fwd P/E 19x — historically cheap for TSM given growth rate; 45% net margins, 35% ROE, -12.2% from 52W high; sector avg fwd P/E for AI semis is 25-30x, so TSM is genuinely undervalued vs peers; geopolitical Taiwan risk discount is real but overstated given customer/US government dependency. • Catalyst: April 16 earnings report — Q1 revenue guidance was strong; AI/HPC demand commentary from CEO will be a key read-through for entire semiconductor complex; recent analyst upgrades (Nomura Taiwan fund +164%) signal institutional accumulation. • Entry: -12.2% from 52W high, consolidating around $341; entering ahead of earnings at historically reasonable valuation; swing trader long at same price confirms entry timing. • Sizing: 2.9% weight — starter position appropriate given earnings risk and cash constraints; will add on pullback or post-earnings confirmation.

PANW.thesis: Palo Alto Networks is the leading AI-native security platform uniquely positioned for the platformization era — consolidating enterprise security spend from 30+ point solutions into a single architecture with superior economics. • Moat: Platform switching costs are extraordinarily high once PANW's Cortex/Prisma suite is integrated across SOC, cloud, and network security; AI-powered Precision AI creates a data moat that improves with scale. • Growth: Revenue growing +15% YoY, transitioning to ARR/subscription model — Remaining Performance Obligations (RPO) growing >20%; cybersecurity TAM expanding to $250B+ as AI increases both threat surface and security spend. • Valuation: Trailing P/E 90.7x on GAAP basis looks elevated, but fwd P/E 41.1x reflects the transition away from product revenue; FCF margins are ~25%+ and the platformization strategy is driving NDR >115%; CEO just bought $10M personally — rare conviction signal. • Catalyst: CEO's $10M personal purchase at ~$155-163 range signals strong insider conviction; cybersecurity sector in secular demand with AI expanding attack surface; earnings approaching where platformization metrics will be key. • Entry: Stock is at $163.21, -27% from 52W high (~$225), technically recovering from correction lows with volume support; insider buy at this level provides price support signal. • Sizing: ~0.8% portfolio weight — intentionally small starter position given cash constraints; max loss if stopped at 12% down is ~$98; sizing small because: (1) portfolio already has significant tech/AI concentration, (2) limited cash available after MRVL/CIEN trims cover this purchase

GLW.thesis: Corning is the dominant supplier of optical fiber, display glass, and specialty materials — a critical infrastructure layer for AI data center interconnects, with the $6B Meta AI fiber deal this week validating the multi-year demand surge. Moat: Corning's optical fiber manufacturing is a vertically integrated oligopoly with 50+ years of process IP, scale advantages that competitors cannot replicate, and long-term supply agreements that create high switching costs — the Meta deal is a 5-year commitment worth $6B. Growth: Revenue growing +20% with AI data center optical fiber demand accelerating; TAM for AI interconnects is massive as every hyperscaler is building out fiber infrastructure to connect GPU clusters; GLW's Optical Communications segment is growing fastest. Valuation: Fwd P/E 37.9x is elevated but reasonable for a company with accelerating 20% revenue growth and improving margins; -8.6% from 52W high suggests the market has not yet fully priced the AI optical thesis. Catalyst: The $6B Meta fiber supply agreement announced this week is the definitive proof point that AI infrastructure buildout is driving sustained multi-year demand for GLW's core product. Entry: +9.3% 1W on the news but still -8.6% from 52W high — momentum positive, not extended at the highs; ATR14 of $9.68 suggests moderate daily volatility. Sizing: 20 shares at ~$148 = ~$2,960, ~2.9% weight — starter position appropriate for a new thesis; not larger given the recent 1W move and position count already at 14.

closed_positions.exit_reason: Mandate change: long-only — legacy short closed at cost
